titleEn: Second screwworm case confirmed miles from first Texas detection
titleZh: 德克萨斯州确认第二例螺旋蝇病例，距首例发现地仅数英里
summaryZh: 美国农业部在德克萨斯州确认了第二例食肉螺旋蝇寄生虫病例，距首例发现地仅5.6英里。新病例出现在扎瓦拉县一头一个月大的小牛身上。该寄生虫通过产卵于动物伤口，幼虫钻入活体组织造成致命伤害。此次疫情对美国养牛业构成重大经济威胁，美国已关闭与墨西哥的活牛进口边境一年多，并投入数百万美元防控。
featuredReason: 该新闻揭示了螺旋蝇疫情对养牛业的重大威胁，智慧畜牧行业可关注如何利用物联网、AI监测和自动化防控技术来应对此类生物安全风险，提升牧场应急响应能力。
contentFull: Health & Disease Notfiable Diseases New World Screwworm Second screwworm case confirmed miles from first Texas detection New infection found in one-month-old calf in same county 8 June 2026 1 minute read By: Global Ag Media North America A second case of the flesh-eating screwworm parasite was confirmed in Texas by the US Department of Agriculture on Friday, emerging just miles from where the first US detection in decades was reported this week, reported Reuters. The new case in Zavala County was detected on a ranch 5.6 miles (9 km) from the first positive case of screwworm in Texas, which the USDA confirmed on Wednesday.The infection, which the USDA said was in a one-month-old calf, was reported earlier on Friday by Reuters, citing sources.The USDA discovered the second infestation "after testing a number of suspect cases," the department's Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service said in a press release.APHIS and Texas Animal Health Commission officials are continuing to "collect and test other samples from the surrounding area which have come back negative," it said.Friday's case and the initial detection in nearby La Pryor, a town roughly 30 miles (50 km) northeast of the US-Mexico border, have dealt a setback to US cattle ranchers, who have been preparing for the arrival of the pest as it has moved north through Mexico over the past year.Screwworms are parasitic flies that deposit eggs in open wounds or mucous membranes of warm-blooded animals. After hatching, the larvae penetrate living tissue, feeding on the host and potentially causing fatal damage if not treated.An outbreak in US border states in the 1960s devastated wildlife and inflicted heavy financial losses on ranchers.A widespread resurgence now could pose a significant economic threat in Texas, the country’s largest cattle-producing state, through animal deaths as well as higher labour and treatment costs.To limit the risk, Washington has kept the US-Mexico border closed to live cattle imports for more than a year and has spent millions of dollars to curb the pest’s northward spread, including funding sterile fly production, expanding trapping programs and stepping up livestock monitoring. Our Partners dsm-firmenich Zoetis FAI Heifer International Hamlet Protein Global Ag Media More from this author More News View all Beef exports to Japan fall 22% in April - USMEF 24 June 2026 1 minute read US beef exports to Korea top $200M in April - USMEF Markets Trade Imports and Exports 24 June 2026 1 minute read Volume down 14% in April as won weakness weighs on demand Cattle futures fall on profit-taking and demand concerns - CME Markets Market Trends Economics 24 June 2026 1 minute read Hog futures mixed as pork prices slide Our Partners dsm-firmenich Zoetis FAI Heifer International Hamlet Protein
translationZh:
据路透社报道，美国农业部周五在德克萨斯州确认了第二例食肉螺旋蝇寄生虫病例，该病例出现在本周早些时候美国报告数十年来首例病例的地点仅数英里之外。

扎瓦拉县的新病例是在距德克萨斯州首例螺旋蝇阳性病例5.6英里（9公里）的一个牧场发现的，美国农业部于周三确认了首例病例。

美国农业部表示，感染发生在一头一个月大的小牛身上，路透社周五早些时候援引消息来源报道了这一消息。

美国农业部动植物卫生检验局在一份新闻稿中表示，该部门在“检测了多个疑似病例后”发现了第二起感染。

动植物卫生检验局和德克萨斯州动物卫生委员会的官员正在继续“从周边地区收集并检测其他样本，这些样本的检测结果均为阴性”。

周五的病例以及最初在拉普赖尔（La Pryor）附近发现的病例，给美国养牛户带来了挫折。拉普赖尔是一个小镇，位于美墨边境东北约30英里（50公里）处。过去一年，随着这种害虫向北穿过墨西哥，养牛户一直在为它的到来做准备。

螺旋蝇是一种寄生性苍蝇，会将卵产在温血动物的开放性伤口或黏膜上。幼虫孵化后，会钻入活体组织，以宿主为食，如果不加以治疗，可能导致致命伤害。

20世纪60年代，美国边境各州爆发的一次疫情摧毁了野生动物，并给牧场主造成了沉重的经济损失。

如今，大范围的疫情卷土重来可能对德克萨斯州构成重大经济威胁。德克萨斯州是美国最大的养牛州，疫情可能导致动物死亡，并增加劳动力和治疗成本。

为降低风险，华盛顿方面已关闭美墨边境的活牛进口超过一年，并投入数百万美元遏制这种害虫向北蔓延，包括资助不育苍蝇生产、扩大诱捕计划以及加强牲畜监测。
contentHtml:
Second screwworm case confirmed miles from first Texas detection 
New infection found in one-month-old calf in same county
